sourceMovie.title: It Was Just an Accident
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: It Was Just an Accident (2025) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Jafar Panahi's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Limey
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If It Was Just an Accident clicked for you, The Limey (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after It Was Just an Accident?
faq.answer: Try The Limey, Cape Fear, Awake—each shares DNA with It Was Just an Accident while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is It Was Just an Accident?
faq.answer: It Was Just an Accident (2025) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Crime, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is It Was Just an Accident worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
